CEI: The boron-containing additive TPFPB induces preferential formation of a steady and smooth interfacial layer on the LNMO cathode, which prevents decomposition of the electrolyte and impedes pulverization of the cathode electrode, leading to a uniform and thin CEI layer of approximately 10 nm that minimizes impedance and allows effective transport of Li ions.
High_voltage_performance_CEI: With the TPFPB additive, the LNMO/Li battery achieved superior capacity retention of 90% after 500 cycles at 0.5C with a charge cut-off voltage of 4.95 V, compared to only 17% capacity retention for the battery without the additive.
SEI: TPFPB is preferentially reduced at the Li anode, forming a dense and smooth SEI layer that suppresses Li dendrite growth and reduces electrolyte decomposition, as evidenced by decreased poly(CO3) signals in XPS spectra and smoother surface morphology in SEM images.

High_voltage_performance_CEI: The battery with the additive (B, F-CSE) shows excellent cycling stability at high voltages, with 97.9% capacity retention after 100 cycles at 4.4V and 99.4% capacity retention after 50 cycles at 4.5V.

CEI: The addition of NHCABT and LiNO3 in the electrolyte enables the formation of a stable and ion-conducting artificial SEI and CEI, rich in inorganic species like LiF, BFx, LixNy, and LixNOy, which enhances oxidation resistance and suppresses electrolyte decomposition at high voltages, as indicated by LSV curves showing a wider electrochemical stability window and characteristic CEI formation signals.
High_voltage_performance_CEI: The Li||NCM811 batteries with NHCABT/LiNO3-CBE exhibit stable cycling at high voltage, delivering a reversible discharge capacity of 138.3 mAh g⁻¹ after 1000 cycles at 1.0 C with an operating voltage up to 4.4 V vs. Li/Li⁺, compared to conventional CBE which suffers from rapid capacity degradation under similar conditions.
SEI: The synergistic effect of NHCABT and LiNO3 induces the formation of a uniform and stable SEI rich in inorganic species such as LiF, BFx, LixNy, and LixNOy, which suppresses side reactions, reduces interface impedance, and enhances Li⁺ conductivity, as evidenced by lower interfacial resistances and improved cycling stability in Li||Li symmetric batteries.
Optimal_operating_temperature: The high-loading Li||LFP batteries with NHCABT/LiNO3-CBE demonstrate stable cycling at low temperatures, delivering specific discharge capacities of 82.8 mAh g⁻¹ at -20°C and well-recovered capacities upon temperature restoration, indicating effective performance across a range of temperatures, though no specific optimal temperature is explicitly stated; the additive enhances low-temperature performance rather than defining a single optimal point.

High_voltage_performance_CEI: The battery with C-LiMCFB additive shows significant improvements in cycling stability at high voltages, such as 4.5V and 4.7V, with better capacity retention compared to batteries without the additive, as evidenced by extended cycle life in half-cells and full-cells under aggressive conditions.

The incorporation of BNNs facilitates the formation of a stable SEI film, suppressing parasitic reactions and reducing interface impedance, as evidenced by XPS analysis showing reduced Li2CO3 formation and the appearance of Li3N, which promotes uniform Li deposition and dendrite suppression.

CEI: A protective cathode electrolyte interface (CEI) film was formed by BDB on the surface of NCM811 in advance to resist HF and solvent erosion, and it has high ionic conductivity and stability, effectively inhibiting solvent decomposition and reducing polarization.
High_voltage_performance_CEI: The battery with 0.7% BDB exhibits a cycle retention rate of 76.5% after 200 cycles at a cut-off voltage of 4.6V, while the battery with baseline electrolyte has a retention rate of only about 54%.
SEI: BDB participates in the formation of a solid electrolyte interface (SEI) film on the lithium metal surface that exhibits high ion conductivity, effectively inhibits the formation of lithium dendrites, reduces interface impedance, and suppresses side reactions.

CEI: The boron-containing additive TBB contributes to the formation of a robust boron-rich cathode electrolyte interphase (CEI) on the NCM811 surface, enhancing structural stability and cycling performance.
High_voltage_performance_CEI: The Li/PDE-TBB/NCM811 cell demonstrates stable performance with a capacity retention of 83.2% after 100 cycles at a high cut-off voltage of 4.3 V, compared to only 58.4% for the cell without TBB.
SEI: TBB promotes the formation of a stable solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) containing abundant polyether derivatives, suppressing side reactions, reducing interface impedance, and inhibiting lithium dendrite growth.

The THB additive promotes the formation of a stable fluorine- and boron-rich SEI to improve interfacial stability and suppress Li dendrite growth, as well as enhancing LiTFSI dissociation and increasing Li+ transference numbers.

CEI: The boron-containing additive LiBF4 forms a robust cathode electrolyte interface (CEI) enriched with boron and fluorine-based inorganic components through sacrificial oxidation, which protects against active material corrosion, suppresses transition metal dissolution, and enhances long-term cycling performance by mitigating electrolyte degradation.
High_voltage_performance_CEI: The battery with the LiBF4 additive shows superior performance at high voltages, with the LNMO cathode achieving a discharge capacity of 115 mAh g⁻¹ and energy density of 540 Wh kg⁻¹ over 500 cycles at a 1C rate, and specific capacities of 124.3 mAh g⁻¹ at 1C and 108.6 mAh g⁻¹ at 2C during rate capability tests, all under operating potentials up to 4.9V. Without the additive, the base electrolyte exhibits lower capacity retention and higher degradation.
SEI: The LiBF4 additive promotes the formation of a stable solid electrolyte interface (SEI) on the lithium metal anode, reducing overpotential, enabling more uniform lithium deposition, lowering interfacial impedance, and improving Coulombic efficiency, which suppresses side reactions and enhances anode stability.

CEI: The boron-containing additive BN-CN, through its cyano-functionalized boron nitride nanosheets, promotes the formation of a stable and dense CEI membrane by optimizing the solvation structure of Li+ and enhancing interactions with TFSI- anions, leading to improved interfacial stability and reduced resistance.
High_voltage_performance_CEI: The BN-CN@QSE electrolyte exhibits an electrochemical stability window of 4.85 V vs. Li+/Li, and Li||NCM811 full cells with high-loading NCM811 cathodes (8.5 mg cm^-2) show excellent cycling performance, retaining a discharge capacity of 135 mAh g^-1 after 400 cycles at 1 C, indicating good high-voltage performance.
SEI: The boron-containing additive BN-CN facilitates the formation of a high-quality SEI by optimizing the solvation structure of Li+, promoting the dissociation of lithium salts, and increasing the proportion of LiF in the SEI, which suppresses side reactions, reduces interface impedance, and homogenizes Li+ fluxes to inhibit dendrite growth.

CEI: TPFPB contributes to the establishment of the cathode electrolyte interphase (CEI) by being oxidized on the cathode surface, forming components such as LiF and Li2CO3 that are electronically insulating and mechanically stable, effectively preventing continuous interfacial side reactions.
High_voltage_performance_CEI: The CPE-3 electrolyte, with TPFPB additive, exhibits stable electrochemical windows exceeding 4.8 V, enabling pairing with high-voltage cathodes like NCA. The NCA/CPE-3/Li cell shows favorable rate capability with discharge capacities of 207.5 mAh g⁻¹ at 0.1 C and 157.9 mAh g⁻¹ at 2 C, and better cycle stability compared to cells without the additive.
SEI: TPFPB facilitates the formation of a stable and even SEI film on the lithium anode, composed of LiF, Li2O, Li2CO3, and BxOy, which suppresses side reactions, reduces interface impedance, and enables homogeneous lithium deposition, leading to improved cycling stability and critical current densities up to 1 mA cm⁻².
Optimal_operating_temperature: The TPFPB-functionalized CPE operates effectively across a wide temperature range from -15°C to 60°C, with ionic conductivities of 4.09 × 10⁻⁵ S cm⁻¹ at -15°C and 6.71 × 10⁻⁵ S cm⁻¹ at 60°C for specific formulations, and maintains excellent battery performance including high discharge capacities and cycle stability at these temperatures.

SEI: The boron moieties in B-SIPE16 contribute to the construction of the SEI, forming a composite structure of organic and inorganic substances that is stable and has high ionic conductivity, with components including LiF and B-containing species.

CEI: TMSPi with the highest HOMO in BGPE contributes to the formation of a stable and homogeneous CEI film, which inhibits the oxidative decomposition of the electrolyte under high voltage and ensures the stability of the cathode structure.
High_voltage_performance_CEI: The NCM/BGPE/Li cell exhibits a superior capacity retention of 82% after 250 cycles at 0.5 C in the voltage range of 3-4.3 V, while the NCM/LE/Li cell presents a failure state after 90 cycles.
SEI: TPFPB with the lowest LUMO is involved in the construction of a composite organic-inorganic SEI rich in B-O and LiF components, which inhibits the growth of Li dendrites and improves the stability of the Li metal anode.

High_voltage_performance_CEI: The all-solid-state Li||AMPE||LiCoO2 cell achieves a cycle life of 700 cycles at 4.40V/0.20C and 200 cycles at 4.50V/0.50C, with a discharge specific capacity of ~180 mAh g⁻¹ at 0.20C for the 4.50V cell.
SEI: The strong interaction between the electron-deficient B and the TFSI⁻ promotes TFSI⁻ decomposition, enabling the formation of a stable anion-derived mosaic-like solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) with Li₂S/LiSO₂F in the outer layer and LiF in the inner layer, which suppresses side reactions and reduces interface impedance.

The boron moiety in BPC could increase the lithium ion transference number compared to the corresponding boron-free SPE due to the anion-trapping capability of boron atom. The formation and growth of lithium dendrites were effectively suppressed by the anion-trapping capability and mechanically stable cross-linked structure of the SPE. Additionally, XPS analysis showed reduced decomposition of TFSI anions on the lithium metal surface in cells with CSPE-B60, indicating a more stable SEI layer with suppressed side reactions and reduced interface impedance.
